prompt:
I'm ripping the routing regexes out of our gateway — every incident lately traces back to one of them. Replace the pile with a real matcher: router.ts exporting class Router.

router.add(pattern, value) registers a route; router.match(path) returns { value, params } for the best route or null. Patterns are /-separated: static segments, :name params, and a trailing * wildcard.

The behavior I need, which test_router.ts locks in (`node --test test_router.ts`):
- params capture single segments and come back percent-decoded ('/users/ada%20l' -> { id: 'ada l' })
- a trailing * matches ONE OR MORE remaining segments, joined back with '/' under params['*']; '*' anywhere but the end of a pattern is an add-time error
- precedence is segment-by-segment, registration order must never matter: static beats param beats wildcard at each step. '/users/me' wins over '/users/:id'; '/a/b/:y' wins over '/a/:x/c' for /a/b/c
- the matcher must backtrack: if the static branch dead-ends deeper in, a param route registered at a shallower level still has to match
- match ignores a query string ('?...') and a trailing slash ('/users/' == '/users'), except the root path '/' itself which is routable
- add throws on a duplicate pattern (same shape twice, param NAMES aside) and on patterns not starting with '/'
- no route -> null, never an exception

Plain TypeScript, node:test, no packages. Tests are read-only as far as you're concerned.
